name: linear-issues-read
description: List and get Linear issues via CLI (read-only operations)
capabilities: List all issues with optional filters by team, project, assignee, or status
Get detailed information for a specific issue by ID or UUID
Output results as formatted tables or JSON for scripting
Resolve team names, user names, and labels to IDs automatically
Limit the number of results returned
author: Bind
securitySummary: Read-only Linear API client using official SDK. Accesses LINEAR_API_KEY for authentication and makes network calls only to Linear's official API. All static findings are false positives - credential access is legitimate for API authentication, network calls go to intended Linear endpoints, and code execution uses only the official SDK.
valueStatement: Quickly view and filter Linear issues without leaving your terminal. Access issue details, search by team or assignee, and get structured output for further processing.
userTitle: Read Linear issues from command line
limitations: Read-only operations only - cannot create, update, or delete issues
Does not support custom issue views or saved searches

useCases.title: Check issue status
useCases.description: Quickly see all open issues for your team without opening the Linear web interface

useCases.title: Find specific issues
useCases.description: Look up issue details by identifier to understand context before starting work

useCases.title: Script issue workflows
useCases.description: Pipe JSON output to other tools for custom reporting or integration workflows

promptTemplates.title: List team issues
promptTemplates.scenario: Get all issues for Engineering team
promptTemplates.prompt: Use linear-issues-read to list issues for team Engineering

promptTemplates.title: Filter by status
promptTemplates.prompt: Use linear-issues-read to show all issues with status In Progress

promptTemplates.title: Get issue details
promptTemplates.prompt: Use linear-issues-read to get details for issue ENG-123

promptTemplates.title: Export for report
promptTemplates.prompt: Use linear-issues-read to list 50 issues as JSON for reporting

bestPractices: Store LINEAR_API_KEY securely in environment variables or .env files, never in source code
Use --json flag when piping output to other command-line tools for reliable parsing
Combine multiple filters like --team and --assignee to narrow down results quickly
antiPatterns: Using this skill for write operations - it is read-only and will fail silently
Hardcoding API keys in scripts or sharing them in chat contexts
Assuming issue IDs are always in ENG-XXX format - some teams use different prefixes

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for reading Linear issues via command line.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of issues I can retrieve?
faq.answer: You can retrieve up to 100 issues by using --limit 100. The default is 25 issues.

faq.question: How do I set up the Linear API key?
faq.answer: Get your key from Linear Settings > API > Personal API Keys and set it as LINEAR_API_KEY in your environment.

faq.question: Is my Linear data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a read-only skill that only calls Linear official API with your credentials. No data stored or transmitted elsewhere.

faq.question: Why am I getting an unknown team error?
faq.answer: Team names are case-insensitive but must match exactly. Use the exact team name from Linear Settings > Teams.

faq.question: How does this compare to using the Linear web app?
faq.answer: This skill provides faster access for common queries and enables scripting. The web app offers more features like drag-and-drop boards.
